description: Rise in Earth's average temperature primarily due to human greenhouse gas emissions since industrial revolution
current_state: 1.2°C above pre-industrial baseline as of 2024; hottest year on record in 2023 and 2024
targets: Paris Agreement: limit to 1.5°C; current policies track toward 2.5-3°C
evidence: Arctic sea ice loss 13% per decade

gases.CO2: most abundant; 420ppm (2024); stays in atmosphere 300-1000 years

gases.methane (CH4): 80x more potent than CO2 over 20 years; from livestock, wetlands, gas

gases.N2O: 300x CO2 potency; from agriculture and fertilizers

concentrations: CO2 highest in 3 million years; CH4 highest in 800,000 years

types.solar PV: fastest growing; costs down 90% since 2010; 580GW installed 2023

types.geothermal: stable baseload; limited to specific regions

solar_growth: IEA: solar will be largest electricity source by 2033
cost_trend: Solar and wind now cheapest new electricity sources in 90% of world
storage: Battery storage capacity doubling every 2-3 years; pumped hydro provides 90% of storage

DACCS: Current cost ~$400-1000/ton CO2; needs to reach <$100 to be economically viable; Climeworks, Carbon Engineering leading
limitation: Even at max deployment, cannot substitute for emissions reductions; supplement only

description: Current extinction rate 100-1000x background rate; termed 6th mass extinction
scale: 1 million species threatened with extinction; 40% of insect species declining
ecosystem_services: Biodiversity provides $33 trillion/year in services (pollination, water purification, climate regulation)
Kunming_Montreal: 2022 global agreement: protect 30% of land and ocean by 2030 (30x30)

description: Economic model eliminating waste by keeping materials in use as long as possible
principles: keep products and materials in use
vs_linear: Linear: take-make-dispose; Circular: reduce, reuse, repair, recycle, recover
potential: Ellen MacArthur Foundation: circular economy could halve emissions by 2030 in key sectors

issues: acidification (30% more acidic since industrial revolution)
plastic pollution (8M tons enter ocean annually)
coral_reefs: 50% of shallow water coral lost since 1950; at 2°C, 99% at risk; critical habitat for 25% of marine species
plastic: Expected to outweigh fish in ocean by 2050 by weight if trends continue
